title: EIP-7906: Transaction Assertions via State Diff Opcode
description: Introduces a TXTRACE opcode that allows contracts to inspect transaction state diffs on-chain, including balance changes, storage slot changes, contract deployments, and emitted events. A companion EVENTDATACOPY opcode copies variable-length event data into memory. Together they let contracts define enforceable assertions about transaction outcomes, protecting users from unintended behavior.
author: Alex Forshtat (@forshtat), Shahaf Nacson (@shahafn), Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh), Yoav Weiss (@yoavw)
laymanDescription: Adds a new instruction to the EVM that lets smart contracts see exactly what a transaction has changed so far, such as balances, storage, and events. Wallets can use this to verify that a transaction only does what the user expects, reducing blind-signing risks and protecting against theft.

endUsers.description: Wallets gain the ability to enforce transaction outcome assertions, significantly reducing blind-signing risk and protecting users from malicious or unexpected contract behavior.

appDevs.description: Developers can build assertion contracts that verify transaction outcomes on-chain, enabling new safety patterns for DeFi protocols and user-facing applications.

walletDevs.description: Wallets can integrate assertion checks that restrict transaction outcomes before signing, offering users verifiable guarantees about what a transaction will do.

toolingInfra.description: Debuggers, simulators, and security tools must support the new TXTRACE and EVENTDATACOPY opcodes for accurate execution tracing and testing.

elClients.description: Execution clients must implement two new opcodes, track cumulative state diffs during transaction execution, and expose them efficiently to the EVM.

clClients.description: No direct impact. The proposal only adds EVM opcodes; consensus clients require no logic changes beyond fork-activation configuration.

benefits: Enables on-chain enforcement of transaction outcome assertions, reducing blind-signing risk.
Provides wallets with verifiable guarantees about what a transaction will change.
Fully backwards compatible, using previously unused opcode slots.
Creates a foundation for ecosystem-wide transaction safety tooling.
tradeoffs: Gas costs for TXTRACE are still TBD, which may affect practical usability.
Incomplete assertions could give users a false sense of security.

improveUX.description: Directly addresses blind-signing risk by allowing wallets to enforce verifiable assertions about transaction outcomes before execution.
